growth_records.medium: Freshwater lake sediment microcosm (Lianhuachi Lake, Beijing) spiked with 13C-bicarbonate + 15N-nitrate

up.units: 13C/12C atom ratio enrichment over time (natural abundance baseline 0.0112; enriched values in Fig. 8, not in text)

up.compound: nitrate (15N, NO3- assimilation as N source)
up.units: 15N/14N atom ratio enrichment over time (natural abundance baseline 0.0037; enriched values in Fig. 8, not in text)

growth_records.cite: Runjia Ji et al. Linking morphology, genome, and metabolic activity of uncultured magnetotactic Nitrospirota at the single-cell level. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01837-6. [PMC11344931]
growth_records.snippet: the stable isotope incubated cells are substantially enriched in 13C- and 15N- isotopes, suggesting the active CO2 fixation and NO3- reduction in LHC-1 cells... these data indicate that LHC-1 is an autotrophic bacterium that produces its ow
growth_records.notes: First single-cell metabolic measurement of an uncultured Nitrospirota MTB. Added-isotope concentrations computed: 50 uL x 1 M NaH13CO3 / 0.025 L = 2 mM 13C-bicarbonate; 112.5 uL x 1 M Na15NO3 / 0.025 L = 4.5 mM 15N-nitrate. Metabolic activity reported ONLY as 13C/12C and 15N/14N atom-ratio enrichment over 23 h (curves in Fig. 8; natural-abundance baselines 0.0112 and 0.0037 given in text). No quantitative uptake/fixation rate in mmol/gDW/h or fmol/cell/h and no growth rate reported, so rates left null. Cell length decreased over first 8 h as C/N incorporated (negative correlation of uptake with length).
